narrative: FY2024-25 is mixed but still favourable for Tata on operating scale. Tata ranked 1st on turnover at ₹132,516.7 crore, ahead of JSW at ₹125,678.0 crore and SAIL at ₹101,716.0 crore, while Jindal Stainless stayed much smaller at ~₹40,816.9 crore. Tata’s BRSR confirms standalone turnover of ₹132,516.66 crore, with steel products contributing 93%, and notes that consolidated turnover was much larger at ₹218,542.51 crore due to India, Europe, Thailand, and other entities. On net worth, JSW is the reported leader because Tata’s FY2024-25 value is missing in the standardised table; this should be treated as a data gap, not proof of weaker financial position. On exports, Jindal Stainless remained the clear leader at 11.69%, supported by stainless-steel global applications and 49 international markets, while Tata improved to 2nd because it held exports stable at 6% and JSW fell to 5.5%.
